phases.name: Backend API - Jazz Standards Endpoints
phases.description: Implement GET endpoints for standards library with TDD. Load standards from JSON file at compile time. Zero database changes.

steps.name: Jazz Standards Data Models
steps.description: Create Rust domain models for jazz standards (JazzStandard struct with id, name, composer, key, dual progressions, metadata). Add Difficulty enum.
criteria: Backend models deserialize all 15 standards from JSON with metadata
Difficulty values restricted to beginner, intermediate, or advanced
Models support JSON serialization and deserialization
Models compile without warnings

steps.name: Jazz Standards Service (TDD)
steps.description: Create service layer loading standards from JSON via include_str! macro. Implements get_all_standards and get_standard_by_id methods.
criteria: Service loads all 15 standards from jazz-standards.json
get_all_standards returns all 15 standards
get_standard_by_id returns Some(standard) for valid ID
get_standard_by_id returns None for invalid ID
Standards loaded at compile time with zero runtime file I/O overhead

steps.name: GET /jazz-standards Endpoint (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR)
steps.description: HTTP handler for listing all standards. Write acceptance test first (RED), implement handler (GREEN), refactor.
criteria: GET /jazz-standards returns 200 with array of 15 standards
Response includes all metadata (name, composer, key, difficulty, tempo, form)
Endpoint requires valid X-API-Key header
Returns 401 without valid API key
Response time <500ms (static data)

steps.name: GET /jazz-standards/{id} Endpoint (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR)
steps.description: HTTP handler for single standard by ID. Write acceptance test first (RED), implement handler (GREEN), refactor.
criteria: GET /jazz-standards/autumn-leaves returns 200 with single standard
Response includes dual progressions (chords_original, chords_improvisation)
Returns 404 for invalid standard ID
Returns 401 without valid API key
ID parameter uses kebab-case (autumn-leaves, blue-bossa)

steps.name: Backend Error Handling & Integration Tests
steps.description: Implement error scenarios (500, 401, 404). Write integration tests covering error paths and authentication.
criteria: 401 error returns message: Missing or invalid API key
404 error returns message: Standard not found
500 error returns message: Failed to load standards
Error responses use consistent JSON format
All backend E2E tests pass (GET /jazz-standards, GET /jazz-standards/{id}, error scenarios)

phases.name: Frontend - React Components and Hooks
phases.description: Implement React components with Outside-In TDD. Create custom hooks for API calls, stateless components for UI. Follow existing patterns.

steps.name: Frontend TypeScript Types
steps.description: Create TypeScript interfaces for jazz standards, CAGED shapes, API requests/responses. Match backend models exactly.
criteria: JazzStandard interface matches backend model
Shape values restricted to exactly five CAGED shapes: C, A, G, E, D
Line interface includes pitches, patterns, tab
GenerateLinesRequest/Response types match existing API contract
Types compile without errors

steps.name: useStandards Hook (TDD with Mocks)
steps.description: Custom hook for fetching all standards. Write unit test with mocked API client first (RED), implement hook (GREEN), refactor.
criteria: Hook fetches standards on mount
Returns loading state during fetch
Returns error state on fetch failure
Provides refetch function for retry
Unit test uses mocked API client (fast execution)

steps.name: useStandardDetail Hook (TDD with Mocks)
steps.description: Custom hook for fetching single standard by ID. Write unit test with mocked API client first (RED), implement hook (GREEN).
criteria: Hook fetches standard by ID on mount or ID change
Returns loading state during fetch
Returns error state on fetch failure (including 404)
Provides refetch function for retry
Unit test uses mocked API client

steps.name: StandardCard Component (TDD with RTL)
steps.description: Display individual standard with metadata. Write component test with React Testing Library first (RED), implement (GREEN).
criteria: Displays standard name, composer, key, difficulty, tempo, form
Difficulty badge color-coded (green=beginner, yellow=intermediate, red=advanced)
Hover effect indicates clickability
Fires onClick callback with standard ID
Accessible with keyboard navigation and ARIA labels

steps.name: DualProgressionDisplay Component (TDD with RTL)
steps.description: Show original and Barry Harris simplified progressions with explanation. Write component test first (RED), implement (GREEN).
criteria: Both progressions labeled clearly (Original Progression, Improvisation Progression)
Explanation text visible below progressions
Responsive layout (side-by-side desktop, stacked mobile)
Chords displayed as badges or inline text
No visual bugs at mobile viewport (320px)

steps.name: ShapeSelector Component (TDD with RTL)
steps.description: CAGED shape buttons with active state. Write component test first (RED), implement controlled component (GREEN).
criteria: Five buttons labeled C, A, G, E, D
Active shape visually distinct with primary color
Disabled during loading (no double-clicks)
Fires onShapeChange callback with selected shape
Accessible with keyboard navigation and ARIA pressed states

steps.name: LineDisplay Component (TDD with abcjs)
steps.description: Render ABC notation via abcjs library. Write component test first (RED), implement rendering logic (GREEN).
criteria: ABC notation renders within 1 second
Chord symbols aligned with notation
Tablature visible below notation (optional)
Responsive width scales to container
Display refreshes when generated lines change

steps.name: StandardsLibraryPage Component (TDD Orchestration)
steps.description: Page component orchestrating standards list. Write component test first (RED), implement with useStandards hook (GREEN).
criteria: Displays 15 standards in grid (3 cols desktop, 1 col mobile)
Loading indicator shown during fetch
Error message with Retry button on failure
Navigates to detail page on standard card click
Grid layout responsive at mobile viewport

steps.name: StandardDetailPage Component (TDD Orchestration)
steps.description: Page component orchestrating detail view. Write component test first (RED), implement with hooks (GREEN).
criteria: Loads standard by ID from URL param
Displays dual progressions immediately
Generate Lines button triggers API call with default E shape
Shape selector appears after first generation
Shape switch triggers regeneration with new shape
Back to Library link navigates to standards list

steps.name: Navigation and Routing Updates
steps.description: Add Experimental tab to navigation. Update router with new routes for standards library and detail pages.
criteria: Experimental tab visible in main navigation
Route /experimental renders StandardsLibraryPage
Route /experimental/standards/:id renders StandardDetailPage
URL params persist on page refresh
Navigation accessible with keyboard (Tab key)

phases.name: Integration, E2E Tests, and Performance Validation
phases.description: Connect frontend to backend with E2E tests. Implement walking skeletons one at a time. Validate all CSF targets with benchmarks.

steps.name: Playwright Setup and Configuration
steps.description: Install Playwright, create config, setup test fixtures. Prepare for E2E test implementation.
criteria: Playwright installed: npm install -D @playwright/test
Chromium browser installed: npx playwright install chromium
playwright.config.ts created with baseURL and webServer config
Test fixtures directory created with mock data
Sample test runs successfully (health check)

steps.name: Walking Skeleton 1: First-Time User Journey (RED-GREEN)
steps.description: E2E test for complete user journey from app open to guitar practice. Enable ONLY this test (mark others @skip). Implement until green.
criteria: User opens app and clicks Experimental tab within 10 seconds
Standards library loads 15 standards within 2 seconds
User selects Autumn Leaves and views dual progressions
User generates lines with default E shape within 3 seconds
User switches to A shape and sees new lines within 3 seconds
Total journey time <30 seconds (CSF 1)

steps.name: Walking Skeleton 2: Returning User URL Access (RED-GREEN)
steps.description: E2E test for direct URL access. Enable this test. Implement until green. Commit on green.
criteria: User opens URL with standard ID (e.g., /experimental/standards/blue-bossa)
Lands directly on detail page without seeing library list first
Dual progressions visible immediately
Generates lines with default E shape within 3 seconds
Switches to G shape and sees new lines within 3 seconds
Total URL-to-practice time <20 seconds

steps.name: Shape Exploration Scenario (RED-GREEN)
steps.description: E2E test for exploring multiple shapes across two standards. Enable this test. Implement until green.
criteria: User explores all 5 shapes (C, A, G, E, D) for Autumn Leaves
Each shape switch completes within 3 seconds
User completes 5 shape switches in <20 seconds total
User returns to library and selects Solar
User explores A and G shapes for Solar
Total time for 2 standards with multiple shapes <2 minutes

steps.name: API Timeout Recovery Scenario (RED-GREEN)
steps.description: E2E test for timeout handling. Enable this test. Implement error handling until green.
criteria: User generates lines for Stella By Starlight
API experiences high latency (>5 seconds)
Timeout message appears: Generation is taking longer than expected
Retry button displayed with loading indicator still visible
User clicks Retry and API responds successfully within 3 seconds
Context preserved (standard and shape not lost)

steps.name: Rate Limit Handling Scenario (RED-GREEN)
steps.description: E2E test for rate limiting. Enable this test. Implement rate limit detection until green.
criteria: User explores shapes rapidly (25 requests in 1 minute)
First 20 requests succeed with 200 OK
21st request returns 429 Too Many Requests
Error message: You're exploring shapes too quickly. Please wait 60 seconds
Countdown timer shows seconds remaining
After 60 seconds, user can continue exploring shapes

steps.name: Standards Library Load Failure Scenario (RED-GREEN)
steps.description: E2E test for library load error. Enable this test. Implement error handling until green.
criteria: User clicks Experimental tab
Standards library API is temporarily unavailable
Loading indicator shown then error message: Unable to load standards library
Retry button displayed without broken UI or stack trace
API becomes available and user clicks Retry
Standards load successfully within 2 seconds

steps.name: Boundary Scenarios: Invalid ID and Missing API Key
steps.description: E2E tests for 404 and 401 error handling. Enable tests. Implement error UI until green.
criteria: User navigates to /experimental/standards/invalid-standard-name
404 error message: Standard not found with Return to Library link
No broken UI or stack trace visible
Unauthenticated API request returns 401 with message: Missing or invalid API key
Authentication enforced consistently across all endpoints

steps.name: Accessibility: Keyboard Navigation Scenario
steps.description: E2E test for keyboard-only navigation. Enable this test. Implement accessibility until green.
criteria: Keyboard-only user can Tab through standards cards
Focus indicators visible on all interactive elements
Enter key on focused standard navigates to detail page
Tab to Generate Lines button and Enter triggers generation
Tab to shape buttons and Enter triggers shape regeneration
All interactive elements have ARIA labels

steps.name: Performance Benchmarks: CSF Validation
steps.description: Implement performance tests validating all 3 CSF targets. Measure and optimize if needed.
criteria: CSF 1 (Frictionless Entry): <30 seconds from app open to generation measured
CSF 2 (Fast Generation): <3 seconds API response p95 validated with 100 requests
CSF 5 (Shape Exploration): <3 seconds per shape switch validated
Performance tests run in CI pipeline
All CSF targets met in >95% of test runs

steps.name: Smoke Tests for CI/CD
steps.description: Create fast smoke tests for post-deployment validation. Target execution time <2 minutes total.
criteria: 9 critical smoke tests (backend health, endpoints, auth, frontend)
4 important smoke tests (caching, rate limiting, error handling)
All tests execute in <2 minutes total
Smoke tests integrated into deploy.yml and deploy-frontend.yml
Smoke tests run automatically after deployment

steps.name: Documentation and Handoff Preparation
steps.description: Update README, create deployment guide, document E2E test execution. Prepare for production deployment.
criteria: README updated with Experimental tab feature description
Test execution commands documented (Playwright, Vitest)
Deployment guide includes wrangler commands and environment variables
All acceptance criteria validated and documented
Feature demo video recorded for stakeholders (optional)

strengths: Clear external validity with system entry point wiring (Steps 01-03, 02-10, 03-02)
Excellent step decomposition ratio (1.59, well below 2.0 threshold)
Walking Skeleton pattern enforces incremental integration (Steps 03-02 through 03-04)
Zero implementation code in roadmap — maintains WHAT vs. HOW separation
Concise acceptance criteria within word count thresholds
E2E tests correctly placed at system boundary (Phase 3)
validation.notes: Roadmap ready for TDD implementation. Follow Outside-In approach: enable ONE acceptance test at a time (mark others @skip), implement until green, commit, repeat. Backend uses real implementations (no mocks). Frontend component unit tests use React Testing Library with user-centric queries (getByRole, getByLabelText) — no shallow rendering or prop assertions. Frontend hooks unit tests use mocked API client. E2E tests use real backend. Estimated delivery: 2-3 weeks (80-100 hours).
